title: Rocket Report: "Panic" over Transporter availability; Isar to launch from Canada
gist: A roundup of launch-industry developments showing SpaceX pulling back its Transporter rideshare from the merchant market, capital raising at record scale (SpaceX IPO, Blue Origin), and super-heavy-lift launch beginning to dictate satellite design.

themes.theme: SpaceX pulling back from the merchant rideshare market

insights.claim: SpaceX is not accepting Transporter rideshare reservations beyond late 2028 or early 2029, and Rocket Lab confirms customers report the same, suggesting SpaceX will refocus Falcon 9 on internal needs like Starlink and its future orbital data centers.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: A SpaceX exit from merchant rideshare opens a capacity gap that competitors like Rocket Lab can capture; watch small-sat launch pricing rise.
insights.quote: There seems to be a panic setting in. There's not as much conviction that Falcon 9 is going to be available for the merchant market out beyond what they've committed to the manifest.
insights.locator: Panic setting in over future of Transporter missions?

insights.claim: Blue Origin is seeking a $10 billion outside investment at a $130 billion valuation, dwarfed by the $85 billion SpaceX raised via IPO earlier this year at a ~$2 trillion valuation.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: The ~15x valuation gap quantifies how far Blue Origin trails SpaceX financially, and its raise is partly to match SpaceX's employee stock options in the talent war.
insights.locator: Blue Origin will seek to raise private capital

insights.claim: The US Space Force added Impulse Space, an in-space propulsion provider rather than a launch company, to its pool of launch bidders, signaling flexibility to meet unmet demand for high-energy orbits.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Kick-stage providers can convert medium-lift rockets into GEO-capable systems, creating a new competitive category in national security launch.
insights.locator: Impulse Space enters military launch competition

insights.claim: For the first time, launch vehicles may be dictating satellite design rather than the reverse, with Starship's 100+ metric ton capacity driving flat-packed, stackable satellite architectures.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: A reversal in the supply-demand balance unlocks new satellite designs and business models; Muon Space's high-power design is an early tell.
insights.locator: Rockets may be starting to dictate satellite design

insights.claim: SpaceX broke its own reuse record by flying booster B1067 for a 36th time and now has more than 10,700 Starlink satellites in orbit, with 635 total booster landings to date.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Deep reuse and constellation scale are the moat metrics underpinning SpaceX's cost advantage and its ability to deprioritize external customers.

insights.claim: Germany's Isar Aerospace will spend about $100 million as a tenant to build a dedicated Spectrum launch complex in Nova Scotia, Canada, targeting launches by 2028 as Ottawa pursues sovereign launch capability.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Sovereign-launch demand is a policy tailwind for European small-launch firms; Canada is buying capability via foreign tenants until domestic boosters mature.
insights.locator: Isar inks deal to launch from Canada

insights.claim: ArianeGroup is testing a 200-kilonewton Vinci upper-stage engine, an ~11% thrust increase, as part of the Ariane 6 Block 2 upgrade package under a 357.6 million-euro ESA program.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Incremental Ariane 6 performance upgrades signal Europe's push to stay competitive in heavy launch, though behind reusable rivals.
insights.locator: ArianeGroup testing more powerful Vinci engine

insights.claim: The final Amazon Leo Atlas V launch (110th overall) closed an era for ULA, with only six Atlas Vs left, reserved for Boeing Starliner missions that NASA cut from six to four guaranteed flights after chronic delays.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Atlas V retirement and reduced Starliner commitments reflect Boeing's troubled crew program and ULA's transition to Vulcan.
insights.locator: What's left for the Atlas V before retirement?
